episodes.title: The Psychology Behind Cults | Nature Boy (Hulu and Hood Horrors) Documentary Review
episodes.description:
#NatureBoy #CarbonNation #carbonnationtv #Hulu #DocumentaryReview #CultDocumentary #RealityTVRecap #MentalHealthAwareness #cultsurvivor  #Documentary #TrueCrimeDocumentary #HuluOriginals #NatureBoydocumentary #tvreview 

The Hulu docuseries The Cult of NatureBoy takes viewers inside the disturbing rise of Eligio Bishop (NatureBoy) and Carbon Nation—a social media movement that evolved into a cult marked by manipulation, coercion, violence, and abuse. In this recap and review, we're breaking down the biggest revelations from the documentary, discussing the psychology behind cult leadership, and sharing our reactions to the survivors' stories.

From the recruitment tactics and social media influence to the allegations of abuse and control, this series raises difficult questions about vulnerability, accountability, and how charismatic leaders gain power over others.

Join us as we unpack the most shocking moments, discuss what the documentary got right (and what may have been left out), and explore the broader conversations around cults, trauma, and recovery.

What did you think of The Cult of NatureBoy? Did the documentary answer your questions, or did it leave you wanting more? Let us know in the comments.

⚠️ Content Warning ⚠️ 

This episode discusses themes that may be distressing to some viewers, including emotional abuse, physical abuse, sexual abuse and exploitation, coercive control, domestic violence, cult dynamics, trauma, and mental health crises. 

Please watch and listen at your own discretion.

If you or someone you know is experiencing abuse, struggling with their mental health, or is in immediate danger, please contact one of the resources above or call emergency services in your area. You deserve support. ❤️

🩵 Mental Health & Survivor Resources
988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
📞 Call or Text: 988
🌐 Website: https://988lifeline.org
Free, confidential support available 24/7 for anyone experiencing emotional distress, suicidal thoughts, substance use concerns, or a mental health crisis.

National Domestic Violence Hotline
📞 Call: 1-800-799-SAFE (7233)
📱 Text: START to 88788
🌐 Website: https://www.thehotline.org
Confidential support, safety planning, and resources for anyone experiencing relationship abuse.

RAINN (National Sexual Assault Hotline)
📞 Call: 1-800-656-HOPE (4673)
📱 Text: HOPE to 64673
🌐 Website: https://rainn.org/hotline
24/7 confidential support for survivors of sexual assault and abuse.

Crisis Text Line
📱 Text HOME to 741741
🌐 Website: https://www.crisistextline.org
Free, confidential crisis support via text message 24/7.

Find a Therapist
🌐 Psychology Today Therapist Directory
https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/therapists
🌐 Therapy for Black Girls Therapist Directory
https://therapyforblackgirls.com/find-a-therapist
